hero.title: Reclaim Your Power. Heal Your Relationship With Money.
hero.subtitle: with Dan Lobel, D.Couns., B.Couns., MCouns&Psych
hero.description: Trauma-informed counselling for financial abuse recovery, economic stress, and nervous-system-aware money work.

about.title: Trauma‑Informed Financial Therapy
paragraphs: Dan is a warm, empathic counsellor who combines contemporary integrative counselling with behavioural psychology to help you understand the “why” behind your feelings and choices — particularly around money, self‑worth, grief, and life transitions.

Therapy is paced by you. Consent, choice, and clear boundaries are central. You will never be asked to disclose more than feels safe. Sessions focus on restoring a felt sense of safety, strengthening self‑trust, and supporting practical next steps at a pace that respects your nervous system.

Drawing on monetary psychotherapy alongside integrative, evidence‑based modalities, Dan supports the emotional, relational, and practical dimensions of financial stress — helping you reconnect with agency, dignity, and hope.

copy.valuePropsEyebrow: How financial trauma therapy with Dan Lobel may benefit you
copy.valuePropsHeading: Dan will concentrate on navigating these challenging constraints alongside you.

valueProps.title: Understanding Financial Trauma
valueProps.description: Financial trauma is deep psychological distress from severe or prolonged money related issues. Many individuals may not initially identify with financial trauma, as the associated behaviours can be subtle and confusing. If you are experiencing distress when it comes to reviewing bills or checking your bank balance, if you find yourself being excluded from financial decisions, if you are experiencing anguish regarding loans or debts, if you are suffering limitations on your access to financial resources, you are probably going through financial trauma.

valueProps.title: Understanding Financial Abuse
valueProps.description: Financial abuse, also referred to as economic abuse, involves an agonising pattern of behaviour where an individual utilises money, finances or financial decisions to exert control, impose restrictions, and torment another person. Financial abuse is one of the main causes of financial trauma. It is important to recognise that financial abuse is overwhelming, debilitating, and causes extreme agony. Understanding and confronting the associated emotions such as shame, guilt, fear or self-doubt are vital for moving forward.

valueProps.title: Financial Trauma & Financial Abuse Impacts
valueProps.description: Clarifying and understanding what's been happening to you without blame or confusion whether it is financial trauma or financial abuse or both. Addressing and acknowledging the emotional and psychological impacts of the trauma or abuse has had or is still having on you. This can include anxiety, depression, PTSD among others. Examining strategies for restoring supportive connections, friends, and family. Restoring self-worth and fostering your confidence. Restoring a sense of safety and choice in your financial decisions.

valueProps.title: Developing Rehabilitation Strategies
valueProps.description: Developing comprehensive strategies to change the current scenario. Engaging with specialised services which may include legal specialists. Formulating strategies for moving forward including educational or career opportunities. Rebuilding your confidence and self-trust around money matters.

valueProps.title: Physical Security and Safety
valueProps.description: Addressing your immediate physical safety concerns. Discussing financial security measures that can be put in place for you. Discussing resources for your independence, housing, food, medical supplies, and other vital necessities. Learning practical tools and boundaries that hold up in real life.

valueProps.title: Cultivating Future Autonomy
valueProps.description: Defining your pragmatic short-term and long-term objectives. Fostering your continuous recovery and ensuring financial stability. Addressing the complexities of debt and legal matters. Making decisions with support and clarity, even when emotions run high.

testimonials.quote: Dan’s presence is calm and respectful. I felt safe naming money shame for the first time.
testimonials.author: Amelia

testimonials.quote: Sessions helped me rebuild self-trust and make decisions from care, not fear.
testimonials.author: Priya

testimonials.quote: We moved at a pace my body could handle. The mix of practicality and kindness was exactly what I needed.
testimonials.author: Grace

faqs.question: Can I start with Telehealth?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most clients begin online. You can shift to in-person or hybrid when it feels right.

faqs.question: Do I need to bring financial documents?
faqs.answer: No. We focus on how money feels emotionally. Bring numbers only if it helps you feel grounded.

faqs.question: Is this therapy appropriate if I’m still experiencing coercion?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We prioritise safety planning and can liaise with other supports, with your consent.

leadMagnet.heading: Need a gentle starting point?
leadMagnet.body: Download the 5-step Financial Safety Check-in used in session to calm your system before money admin.
leadMagnet.helper: Requesting the guide acknowledges the consent statement in our client agreement.
